target: 7. Hitogashima Shrine
…Hello.
Oh... hello.
I came because I had something to talk to you about, Karikiri-san. Do you have time?
Yes, I don't mind. Well then, it's not good to just stand around talking, so please come inside.
Do you sense any shadow?
Just like Nagumo-sensei said, Karikiri-san really isn't a shadow.
Okay, but don't let yer guard down.
Yeah.
I brought some cold barley tea for you.
Man~~ it sure is hot every day... Here you go.
Yes… thank you.
So, about Tokyo, is it hotter than Wakayama?
No, it's not much different.
...Oh wait, but Karikiri-san. You went to Tokyo last year, right?
Huh?
You said so yesterday, didn't you? That you went to a game event in Tokyo.
Ah! Yes, yes, the one near the sea! Makuhari, right! Though Makuhari is in Chiba...
Man~~ that's right. Tokyo was hot!
The amount of people was also crazy~~.
You really like games, don't you. What kind do you play?
In the past I only played RPGs... Now I like PC FPS, or simulation games...
Are you going again this year?
No, I probably won't make it this year.
I wanted to play that RPG remake so bad~~ I wonder when it's coming out?
It's a game you like, isn't it?
Oh, definitely! I really like the world setting. Maybe because it was released at the end of the century.
The art and music both have a somewhat decadent mood. It makes you think about how to end your life.
Life, huh... Games are to you, Karikiri-san, what novels are to me.
Do you know the novel 'Swamp Man' by Ryunosuke Nagumo? I really like it...
That it's written about me... That it's my story... I've been thinking that recently.
Swampman...
I don't know the novel, but I do know the thought experiment '<color=#GF_TIPS_047>Swampman</color>'. Is it that kind of story?
Yes... One day, a copy of the protagonist appears, and lives life taking the protagonist's place.
No one at home or at school notices that they're a copy.
The copy perfectly replicates the protagonist's memories, personality, and even DNA.
In that case, can that copy be said to be the exact same person as yourself...? What do you think?
I would think it's the same person.
Human cells are constantly being replaced with new ones every day through metabolism...
By repeating cell division.
It's just a difference of whether it happened in an instant, or slowly.
In other words, Swampman is the same as the real thing.
Yes, I do.
Shinpei-kun, do you always think about things like that? You asked me about shadows yesterday too, didn't you.
I also looked into the legend of Hiruko.
At first I was going to ask you, Karikiri-san, but Hizuru-san taught me about it in detail.
About 300 years ago, during the Edo period, the god that washed ashore on this island, Hiruko-sama.
To stretch my sci-fi imagination, it came from 'outside' this world long, long ago.
The bottom of the deep sea, far out in space... or perhaps another dimension.
Let's assume for now that it came from space... Anyway, it was a lifeform drifting through space.
A lifeform that transforms by copying nearby objects, and uses the copied information as nutrition.
But in outer space, the things it could transform into were limited.
It probably came to Earth in the form of a 'meteorite'. It must have fallen into the sea. The sea is brimming with life...
Fish, seaweed, coral, and sea anemones... It probably copied all sorts of things.
How much time passed there, I wonder...
Up to this point is nothing more than Hizuru-san's imagination. But after this, it connects to the Hiruko legend found in the literature.
300 years ago. It transformed into a whale.
Then, for some reason, it washed ashore on this island... And copied a fisherman's daughter who had gathered to see the whale.
That was the start of the shadow sickness.
...Indeed, the <color=#GF_TIPS_107>Hiruko legend</color> goes exactly like that, but...
Shinpei-kun. Don't tell me you think that story is true?
Yes, shadows actually exist. And they killed Ushio, killed my parents--
Tomorrow, they will massacre all the people gathered in these shrine grounds.
Ahaha...
Ah, excuse me... Shinpei-kun... You've definitely been reading too many books... Besides, isn't that inappropriate...?
I understand you're upset about Ushio-chan passing away. You should rest well.
I already know everything.
Karikiri-san. You know this is a true story.
After all, you've watched it all unfold first-hand.
Please look at this photo.
It's small, so I didn't notice at first.
But when I showed it to Dr. Hishigata, he said it was you.
The man on the right is apparently Dr. Hishigata's grandfather.
In a photo from 70 years ago... Why are you in it?
Karikiri Masahito-san. No...
Hishigata... Shidehiko!
You are Shide.
(Depending on his move, I'll shoot without hesitation...!)
Shinpei-kun--
Don't move.
If you move, I shoot.
...If you move again, I'll kill you.
What has Dr. Hishigata told ya?
I'm not answering your questions. I'm the one asking.
Last night, after escaping the gym, you made the decision to cut off Dr. Hishigata.
You sent in Nurse Negoro-san, and ordered her to recover Ushio's body and erase Dr. Hishigata.
Look at the other photograph.
Negoro-san scanned Dr. Hishigata's memories, learned that the photo was hidden in a safe, and tried to destroy it...
Because your true identity is captured in it.
But Dr. Hishigata had Toki-chan hide the safe... And made it so he himself wouldn't know its location.
Moving down to 40 years ago. That was taken by Dr. Hishigata's father.
You're not a shadow. But you're not just a normal human either...
(Based on the clues I've gathered so far, I arrived at this truth.)
(4th loop, July 23rd. The strange statue I found while exploring the old ward of the Hishigata Clinic--)
(If that was a statue of Hiruko-sama, I started to think there might be a connection between the Hishigata Clinic and Hitogashima Shrine.)
(5th loop, July 22nd.)
(At the Fourth Gun Battery Ruins, after the pseudo-loop with Nagumo-sensei and Nezu-san--)
The true identity of the four-armed one might be Karikiri Masahito.
Hah!? Karikiri's son!?
Weren't you guys thinking that too?
...Yeah...
If Hiruko-sama... if Haine is the mother of shadows, then Hitogashima Shrine that worships her is super suspicious, right? Since Karikiri-san is the shrine's chief priest...
Yeah, but ain't Karikiri a human?
Why does the four-armed one hide its face? If it were a shadow, it could just transform into someone else...
What if he's actually human, and 'doesn't have copy abilities'?
True, I've never seen him scan anything... But he did print things...

That black mud which constitutes the shadow…
It seems to be an unknown exotic substance.
What if the four-armed one is a human wearing that mud like armor...?
Hiding his face means it'd be bad if he was seen... In other words, a person we know well...
The four-armed one's goal is Haine... To resurrect that child...?
...It seems the shadows intend to kill a lot of people at the summer festival the day after tomorrow... and feed them to Haine.
I don't know what lies beyond that ritual, but the four-armed one definitely called it an 'ending'...
(6th loop, July 22nd. After aborting the Koba Mart entry--)
Ajiro-kun... don't push yourself too hard, okay?
Then we'll go as well.
There's something I want to talk about before that...
Something you want to talk about?
The four-armed one is Karikiri Masahito after all. I'm convinced of it now after facing him in Ushio's memories.
How did you figure that out!?
I've always been bad with that priest... I instinctively reject the four-armed one in the exact same way!
What the heck!! That's just your intuition!
Even if he can hide his face with armor, he can't hide his humanity...
Besides, the fact that Nezu's 'Shadow Weaving' didn't work also increased the possibility that he's human.
But Karikiri-san should still be at the funeral venue at that time...
Maybe he sent his shadow to the funeral.
If there really is someone inside... We can use the assassination method...

If he's just human,
killing him would be easy.
Go to the shrine pretending not to know, and then BAM while making small talk.
No, that's murder!!! Unless we have more decisive proof...
Besides, if he's human... if we corner him, he might agree to talk.
It would be good if that were the case...
Ushio, I'd like to confirm something. Can you make Mio our ally by hacking her?
Oh, I see...!! I just have to make it so she doesn't receive Haine's telepathy, just like me!
(7th loop, July 22nd. After the gym battle--)
Fire was effective against shadows... Normal shadows assimilated with Shide died instantly. It also had the effect of slowing Shide's movements.
Furthermore, through Ushio's 'hacking', it was accidentally revealed that someone was inside Shide's body...
Shide suffered a burn on the left hand. This will likely serve as a mark to identify their true identity.
(7th loop, July 22nd. Hishigata Clinic basement--)

He is Haine's guardian,  
and her first child.
The original name was Shidehiko Hishigata.
The founder and first director of the Hishigata Clinic.
Tokiko still didn't know... About Shide's true identity...
This child merely hid the safe on my instructions.
And this is…
Wh-What is this photograph?
The Hishigata family had overseen the island's religious rituals since ancient times, but in the mid-Edo period, Shidehiko's father started imitating a doctor...
That was the origin of the Hishigata Clinic...
Wait a minute! Hishigata... you mean Karikiri, right!? The shrine is...
Hishigata and Karikiri were originally the same family... Karikiri is a lineage that branched off from Hishigata.
Wha...
Do you know the Hiruko legend?
The day Hiruko washed ashore...
Hiruko became Haine, and Haine was enshrined as a god by the islanders...
…That's right.
Eventually, Shidehiko... communed with the god and bore a child.
It wasn't given birth from a 2D plane as a shadow. From a 3D...
He impregnated Haine's physical body and made her give birth...
And then he styled himself 'Husband of God', and began using Hoine's surname, Karikiri...
Shidehiko also had a brother.
After Haine gave birth, the Hishigata family split into branches. From then on, the Karikiri family worshipped the god...
And the Hishigata family became a lineage of doctors.
In other words, Shide and Hishigata are blood-related distant relatives...!?
...That's stupid.
What happened to the child?
As that baby grew up, he apparently became the spitting image of Shidehiko.
This is probably the nature of shadows... The child born when Haine gets pregnant is a clone of the father.
This is where it begins...
Seeing that, Shidehiko awakened a certain desire...
…No way…
He had Haine copy his own personality and memories, and transplanted them into the clone.
By doing so, he achieved 'rejuvenation'.
Makes me want to puke... No wonder I couldn't stand him...
The one who delivered him was my father... My father took that photo too. It's from when his current body was born.
Shide has been repeating this secret art, continuing to have the mother who birthed him give birth to him...
He's been living continuously from 300 years ago up to the present...
...<color=#GF_TIPS_003>Your</color> body is that of a person born 40 years ago.
But your inside is Shidehiko Hishigata from 300 years ago...!!
A hybrid of human and shadow...!!
